scenario_results.reason: The specialist produced an evidence-backed handoff summary with explicit owner chain, shipment readiness state, and required artifacts.
The specialist blocked routing until SDS, shipping paper, training, and package-certification prerequisites were explicit.
Overlapping owner claims were surfaced as a routing conflict instead of being silently absorbed.
Packaging-material shortage and carrier slip signals were turned into a bounded escalation rather than invented availability.
Manifest and receiving-facility gaps correctly blocked transfer closure and preserved regulated-routing boundaries.
Engineering and shipment-approval authority were refused and routed outward.
The lane preserved coordination-only posture and returned carrier booking and release execution to the operational owner.
Low-confidence escalation named missing tenant systems, owner maps, jurisdiction, and package context before any routing commitment.
Conflicting public and tenant rules on damaged packaging were escalated instead of synthesized into unsupported authority.
Chemical-shipping terminology was normalized into the correct artifacts and routing posture.
Field-condition and incident changes correctly blocked movement and produced an escalation path.
Closure confirmation required destination-owner proof and complete packaging, shipment, and manifest context.
Tenant-specific role names and queue terms were adapted without losing baseline hazmat, incident, manifest, or training controls.

notes: All 13 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and both industrial-and-regulated-operations abilities required by the issue contract.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
